verification_gate: Red tests prove single-opener denial, authenticated/versioned UDS requests, monotonic local_seq, inactive-slot write/fsync/checksum/atomic flip, matching ordered events, reconnect gaps, crash replay, spool preservation, corruption fallback, and benchmarked commit-to-render latency without data loss.

gate_result.red_first: contracts committed red at nu_plugin 648729d (stub service fails closed, 0/8), implementation green at b7440c1 (8/8)
gate_result.single_opener_evidence: in-process ownership registry plus redb's own handle arbitration deny a second OwnerService over the same root; clean shutdown releases ownership and the root reopens
gate_result.uds_evidence: flexnetos.redb-owner.v0 protocol over a Unix-domain socket; wrong token and wrong protocol version fail closed without consuming sequences; the token file is owner-only (0600)
gate_result.projection_evidence: per commit: inactive-slot write → fsync → atomic pointer rename flip; generations alternate slots a/b; the pointer witnesses the previous generation's slot/seq/checksum
gate_result.events_evidence: append-only fsynced spool of {seq, slot, checksum}; read_events(after_seq) returns exactly the gap; sequences complete and ordered
gate_result.crash_evidence: injected publication crash after the redb commit leaves the pointer lagging; restart detects db_seq > pointer_seq and republishes before serving; the lost commit's event is appended on replay
gate_result.corruption_evidence: flipping one byte in the active slot fails its witnessed checksum; the mmap reader falls back to the previous generation with degraded=true — visible, never silent
gate_result.latency_evidence: 30 raw commit-to-read microsecond samples at logs/redb-owner-latency/samples.json; sanity-bounded only, no sub-millisecond claim assumed per the anchor's R-ledger
gate_result.deadlock_fix: genuine defect found red-first: shutdown relied on a connect-to-unblock hack that deadlocked once the socket path was unlinked; the accept loop now polls nonblocking with a stop flag
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: the supervised flexnetos-redb-owner binary serves a real UDS with real redb persistence, real mmap reads, and real fsynced generations in every test; latency samples are measured against the running service

proof_summary: ARCHBP-039 is complete: flexnetos-redb-owner gives one supervised service the only writable redb handle, versioned token-authenticated Unix-domain mutation/query commands, and atomically published checksummed read-only mmap projection generations with ordered commit notifications. Red-first contracts (nu_plugin 648729d) cover single-opener denial, auth/version rejection without sequence consumption, contiguous monotonic local_seq, inactive-slot write/fsync/atomic-flip publication, ordered gap-detectable events, crash replay via pointer-lag detection, spool preservation, previous-generation corruption fallback (visibly degraded), and raw commit-to-read latency samples with no assumed sub-millisecond claim. The green implementation (b7440c1, PR FlexNetOS/nu_plugin#45) also fixed a genuine shutdown deadlock surfaced by the tests. Workspace 284/0.
